rulings.Case Title: DANIEL BOREEN VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Plaintiffs Failure To Comply With Discovery Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. Defendant Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions For Plaintiffs Failure To Comply With Discovery Order is GRANTED as requested. Sanctions granted in the amount of $553. No opposition filed. Moving party to prepare order. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: 547-551 HAYES LLC. A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. PUBLIC BIKES, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment As To Public Bikes
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2019 line 2. PLAINTIFF 547-551 HAYES LLC. A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY Application For Right To Attach Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment As To Public Bikes is OFF CALENDAR. On November 26, 3019 this Court issued a tentative ruling indicating that there is no valid proof of service on file submitted in accordance with CRC 3.1300(c). After November 27, 2019 hearing, the hearing was continued. There is still no valid proof of service on file. Proof of Service filed on November 26, 2019 indicates only that "Anita Vejar" gave an envelope containing application papers to an unknown individual to be delivered to Defendant's counsel. No proof of service executed by the server is on file. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CANDIDA SPIELBAUER TRUST VS. TIRUMALAI TEJAS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 12, 2019 line 8. DEFENDANT TIRUMALAI TEJAS DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to either (1) allege a relationship between Plaintiff and Defendants giving rise to a claim for unlawful detainer, e.g. landlord-tenant; licensor-licensee; employer-employee; and/or (2) allege facts is support of a cause of action for forcible entry/forcible detainer. If Plaintiff asserts a claim under the first option, Plaintiff shall also allege compliance with or exemption from SFRO. Further, it appears to the Court, based on the current allegations in the complaint and opposition, that Plaintiff's cause of action against the Defendants for possession hinges on a determination that an existing purported lease for the property was not legally binding and/or is a forgery. Plaintiff shall address in any amended complaint if such a determination is being sought as a condition precedent to Plaintiff's claim that Defendants are squatters/trespassers and whether such a determination can be made in the absence of all parties to the purported lease. =(501/CFH)
